questions.subject: NSEP
questions.q: A simple pendulum consisting of a small bob of mass m attached to a massless inextensible string of length $\ell$, hanging vertically from the ceiling, is oscillating in a vertical plane with an angular amplitude $\theta_m$ such that the maximum tension in its string is three times the minimum tension in the string i.e., $\mathrm{T}_{\max }=3 \mathrm{~T}_{\min }$. The correct option(s) is/are
options: The maximum tension in the string is $\mathrm{T}_{\max }=\mathrm{mg}\left(3-2 \cos \theta_{\mathrm{m}}\right)$
The maximum tension in the string is $T_{\max }=\frac{9}{5} m g$
The maximum velocity of the bob on its way is $v_{\max }=3.96 \mathrm{~ms}^{-1}$
The angular amplitude $\theta_{\mathrm{m}}$ lies in the range $\frac{\pi}{4} < \theta_m < \frac{\pi}{3}$
